Package org.eventb.core.ast

Interface IParseResult

All Superinterfaces:
IResult
All Known Implementing Classes:
ParseResult
public interface IParseResult extends IResult
Common protocol for reporting results of the AST parser.

The method described below give access to the result of the parser, i.e., the tree that represents the parsed formula.

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    Returns the formula factory used for parsing.

    getProblems, hasProblem

  • Method Details

    • isSuccess

      @Deprecated boolean isSuccess()
      Deprecated.
      Use !hasProblem() instead
      Returns whether parsing was successful. However, this method will return true in case of characters ignored by the lexical analyzer. Its usage is thus discouraged in favor of a test for !hasProblem() which will return false in case of lexical error.
      Specified by:
      isSuccess in interface IResult
      Returns:
      true iff parsing succeeded
      See Also:
      • IResult.hasProblem()

    • getParsedAssignment

      Assignment getParsedAssignment()
      Returns the parsed assignment.

      A null result is returned in the following cases (exhaustive list):

      • errors were encountered during parsing,
      • the parser was not configured for parsing an assignment.

      Returns:
      the parsed assignment or null in case of error

    • getParsedExpression

      Expression getParsedExpression()
      Returns the parsed expression.

      A null result is returned in the following cases (exhaustive list):

      • errors were encountered during parsing,
      • the parser was not configured for parsing an expression.

      Returns:
      the parsed expression or null in case of error

    • getParsedPredicate

      Predicate getParsedPredicate()
      Returns the parsed predicate.

      A null result is returned in the following cases (exhaustive list):

      • errors were encountered during parsing,
      • the parser was not configured for parsing a predicate.

      Returns:
      the parsed predicate or null in case of error

    • getParsedType

      Type getParsedType()
      Returns the parsed type.

      A null result is returned in the following cases (exhaustive list):

      • errors were encountered during parsing,
      • the parser was not configured for parsing a type.

      Returns:
      the parsed type or null in case of error

    • getFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Returns the formula factory used for parsing.
      Returns:
      the formula factory used
      Since:
      3.0
